meta.toolResultPreview: 1: # ussycode 2: 3: Self-hosted dev environment platform for the Ussyverse. Instant SSH-accessible microVMs with persistent disks, automatic HTTPS, and AI agent support. 4: 5: **`ssh ussyco.de`** -- get a dev environment in seconds. No signup. No credit card. SSH keys are identity. 6: 7: ## What is this? 8: 9: ussycode gives anyone instant dev environments via SSH. Under the hood it provisions Firecracker microVMs with ZFS-backed persistent storage, automatic HTTPS via Caddy wildcard TLS, and a built-in metadata service with LLM proxy access. 10: 11: Anyone can contribute compute to the **Ussyverse Server Pool** by deploying a single agent binary on their own hardware. vision.rationale: The repository explicitly states its vision in README, spec, and community docs: instant SSH-accessible dev environments, free/community compute, persistent disks, automatic HTTPS, and AI-agent support for the Ussyverse.

architecture.rationale: Architecture is thoroughly documented with diagrams and component descriptions spanning the control plane, SSH gateway, VM manager, Firecracker microVMs, storage, proxying, scheduler, mesh networking, and agent nodes.

principles_guidelines.rationale: There are strong process and convention sources: the orchestrator prompt, spec, dev plan, handoff notes, and community docs define parallel-track execution, build/test loops, progress files, commit formats, and security/ops expectations.
